question: Consider the two-dimensional classical icosahedron spin model on the square lattice. Each lattice site carries a unit vector spin that can point to one of the 12 vertices of a regular icosahedron. Neighboring spins interact ferromagnetically with Hamiltonian H = -J * sum_{<i,j>} n_i . n_j, where n_i and n_j are unit vectors pointing to icosahedron vertices and the dot product is their inner product, with coupling constant J = 1. What is the critical temperature T_c of the single continuous order-disorder phase transition, in units of J/k_B (i.e., k_B T_c / J)? Output a JSON object {"kBTc_over_J": <value>}.

metadata.system: 2D icosahedron spin model on square lattice, J=1
metadata.source_excerpt: exhibits a single continuous (second-order) order-disorder phase transition at the critical temperature T_c = 0.5550 +/- 0.0001; below this temperature the low-temperature phase breaks the full icosahedral symmetry down to a fivefold axial symmetry.
metadata.difficulty_note: 2D icosahedron (12-state discrete orientation) spin model on square lattice, CTMRG method
